hero.subtitle: Your Campus Survival Guide
hero.description: A student-created resource to help international students navigate Seattle and UW campus life. Find housing, discover great food, and make the most of your Husky experience.

heroBanner.subtitle: From banking to OPT, HuskyGuide guides every step of your journey at UW.

about.title: Made by Huskies, for Huskies
about.subtitle: We know how overwhelming it can be to start fresh in a new country. That's why we created this guide.
about.byStudentsDesc: Created by international students who've been through it all
about.communityDesc: Recommendations based on real experiences and feedback
about.updatedDesc: Fresh tips and information added regularly
about.contactPrompt: Have tips to share or suggestions for improvement?

footer.description: A student-created resource helping international students navigate UW Seattle and make the most of their Husky experience.
footer.madeBy: Made by UW students, for UW students 💜
footer.suggestions: Have suggestions or corrections? We'd love to hear from you!
footer.copyright: UW Survival Guide. All information provided as-is.
footer.notAffiliated: Not officially affiliated with the University of Washington.
footer.disclaimerText: This guide is created by students and is for informational purposes only. Information may be outdated or inaccurate. Always verify important details with official UW sources. We are not responsible for any decisions made based on this information.

campusLife.title: Campus Life Essentials
campusLife.subtitle: Everything you need to know to hit the ground running at UW. From your first week tasks to emergency contacts.

firstWeek.title: First Week Checklist
firstWeek.subtitle: Complete these tasks in your first week to set yourself up for success. High priority items first!
firstWeek.huskyCardDesc: Your official UW ID. Needed for building access, library, gym, transit, and more. Get it at the Husky Card Office in Schmitz Hall.
firstWeek.upassDesc: Free unlimited transit on buses and light rail! Activate through your MyUW account within the first week.
firstWeek.myuwDesc: Your portal for class registration, grades, email, and all UW services. Use your UW NetID to log in.
firstWeek.classroomsDesc: Do a campus walk-through before classes start. The UW campus is big – know where you're going!
firstWeek.clubsDesc: Over 1,000 clubs at UW! Check out the Student Activities Office or attend the RSO Fair to find your community.

scenery.title: Campus Scenery
scenery.subtitle: Some beautiful spots on campus you'll want to explore and photograph.
scenery.suzzalloDesc: The iconic "Harry Potter" library with stunning Gothic architecture
scenery.redSquareDesc: The heart of campus with views of Mount Rainier on clear days
scenery.mapSubtitle: Interactive map to help you find buildings, parking, and more.

locations.title: Essential Campus Locations
locations.subtitle: Know where to study, work out, get help, and grab coffee. These are the spots you'll visit most.
locations.odegaardDesc: 24/7 undergraduate library with group study rooms, computer labs, and the popular UW Starbucks.
locations.suzzalloDesc: The iconic 'Harry Potter' library. Beautiful reading room, quiet study spaces, and research resources.
locations.imaDesc: State-of-the-art gym with pools, climbing wall, basketball courts, and fitness classes. Free for students!
locations.espressoDesc: Cozy café in Suzzallo Library. Great coffee and snacks for study sessions.
locations.hubDesc: Student center with food court, study lounges, bowling alley, and student org offices.
locations.labsDesc: Multiple labs across campus with printing, scanning, and software. Odegaard and Mary Gates Hall are popular.
locations.healthDesc: Primary care, mental health, pharmacy, and health education. Most services covered by student insurance.
locations.allenDesc: North wing has dedicated quiet floors. Perfect for focused studying and research.

apps.title: Useful Apps & Resources
apps.subtitle: Download these apps and bookmark these sites. They'll make your UW life much easier.
apps.myuwAppDesc: Access classes, grades, campus map, and bus schedules on the go.
apps.safeCampusDesc: Report safety concerns, access emergency resources, and get real-time alerts.
apps.transitDesc: Real-time bus arrivals and trip planning for Seattle transit.
apps.wechatDesc: Connect with other international students. Search 'UW Class of [Year]' groups.
apps.canvasDesc: Where professors post assignments, lectures, and grades. Check daily!
apps.issDesc: International Student Services portal for visa info, events, and resources.

emergency.title: Emergency Contacts
emergency.subtitle: Save these numbers in your phone. You might never need them, but it's important to have them.
emergency.policeDesc: 24/7 campus police for emergencies. Call 911 for life-threatening emergencies.
emergency.hallHealthDesc: Medical appointments and health advice during business hours.
emergency.issContactDesc: Visa questions, travel signatures, and international student support.
emergency.counselingDesc: Free mental health counseling for students. Crisis support available.
emergency.safeCampusLineDesc: Report concerns, get advice, or talk to someone any time.

circle.title: CIRCLE - International Student Resources
circle.subtitle: The Center for International Relations & Cultural Leadership Exchange (CIRCLE) offers programs and support specifically for international students.
circle.coachingDesc: One-on-one appointments to identify areas of growth and barriers to success and well-being.
circle.friendsDesc: A space where international students can hang out, make friends, get support, and much more!
circle.uniteUWDesc: Cultural exchange program that builds bridges between domestic and international students.
circle.iwpDesc: Programs held at the start of every quarter to help new international students transition to UW life.
circle.leadershipDesc: Connect with prominent UW alumni from around the world across different industries.
circle.podcastDesc: Stay informed on academic success, career development, and UW resources.
circle.visitSubtitle: Learn more about all programs and sign up for events.

proTip.title: Pro Tip for International Students
proTip.content: Visit the International Student Services (ISS) office in Schmitz Hall during your first week. They can help with visa questions, cultural adjustment, and connecting you with other international students.

housing.title: Housing Guide
housing.subtitle: Find safe neighborhoods, discover student-friendly apartments, and learn what to look for when renting in Seattle.

neighborhoods.title: Safe Neighborhoods
neighborhoods.subtitle: Popular areas near UW where students typically live. Each has its own character and trade-offs.

apartments.title: Popular Student Apartments
apartments.subtitle: These are commonly recommended by students. Prices are estimates and may vary.

tips.title: Rental Tips
tips.subtitle: What to know before signing a lease. These tips can save you from common problems.

housing.disclaimer: This information is based on student experiences and is for reference only. Prices, availability, and conditions may change. Always verify details independently before making any decisions.

food.title: Food & Dining
food.subtitle: Discover the best eats near UW campus. From quick bites between classes to weekend brunch spots.

proTips.title: Pro Tips for Eating Near Campus
proTips.huskyCardDesc: Many U-District restaurants offer 10-15% student discounts. Always ask!
proTips.peakHoursDesc: Popular spots get crowded 12-1pm. Eat early or late for shorter waits.
proTips.checkAppsDesc: Use DoorDash, Uber Eats, or Snackpass for student deals and free delivery.

food.disclaimer: Prices, hours, and offerings may change. Some restaurants may be temporarily closed. We recommend checking Google Maps or calling ahead before visiting.

transport.title: Getting Around Seattle
transport.subtitle: Your Husky Card is your free transit pass. Buses, trains, streetcars – all included with your tuition!
transport.saveTip: Save this page - screenshot the routes you use most!

upass.title: Your U-Pass = FREE Unlimited Transit!
upass.description: As a UW student, your Husky Card works as an unlimited transit pass. It covers King County Metro buses, Link Light Rail, Sound Transit, Seattle Streetcar, and Water Taxi.

gettingToCampus.title: Getting to Campus
gettingToCampus.subtitle: How to reach UW from major locations
gettingToCampus.airportTip: Take Link Light Rail (1 Line) directly to UW Station
gettingToCampus.downtownRail: Light Rail: Westlake → UW Station (15 min)

routes.title: Popular Student Routes
routes.subtitle: Routes you'll use most often – save these for quick reference!

apps.title: Essential Apps
apps.subtitle: Download these before your first day

quickRef.title: 📌 Quick Reference
quickRef.subtitle: Important contacts and info at your fingertips

tips.title: Tips for New Students
tips.subtitle: Transit etiquette and safety advice
tips.tapDesc: Tap your Husky Card when boarding buses and at Light Rail stations. It's free but required!
tips.stopDesc: Pull the yellow cord or push the red button near your stop. The bus won't stop automatically.
tips.offlineDesc: Save bus schedules and maps offline for areas with poor signal.
tips.nightDesc: Stick to well-lit routes after dark. Use NightRide shuttle or travel with friends.
tips.bikeDesc: Lime bikes are scattered around campus. Great for short trips when the bus isn't coming.
tips.awareDesc: Keep one earbud out and stay alert, especially at night or in unfamiliar areas.

shuttles.title: Campus Shuttle Services
shuttles.subtitle: Free shuttles operated by UW Transportation

firstWeekTip.title: First Week Tip
firstWeekTip.content: Activate your U-Pass through MyUW before your first bus ride. It takes 24-48 hours to activate. If you need to travel before activation, single-ride fares are $2.75 (pay with ORCA or credit card).

chat.browseOrSignIn: Browse general resources or sign in for a personalized experience
chat.welcomeMessage: Hi! I'm HuskyGuide 🐾. I can help you explore UW resources. Log in to get advice tailored to your specific program and budget!
chat.guestReply: Hi! I'm HuskyGuide. Log in to get advice tailored to your specific program and budget! In the meantime, feel free to browse our guides above. 🐺
chat.signInCta: Sign in to save your progress and get tailored advice for your program, budget, and quarter.
chat.safetyDisclaimer: 🔒 HuskyGuide AI will never ask for your SSN or passwords. Please do not share sensitive ID numbers.
